metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `cohort` value for `pass1_isf` findings (e.g., `["ISF"]`) and for `pass1_container` findings (e.g., the sorted `valid_ids` list like `["G3","G4"]`) in the plan, so the AC1 jq proof has an unambiguous expected value.
findings.finding: The plan never defines what `cohort` means for `pass1_isf` findings. ISF does not receive a multi-criterion chunk — it runs as a single fixed call with no chunk composition (passes.py:409-461 hardcodes `"criteria": ["ISF"]` with no `chunk` parameter). The 'sorted criterion-id set' definition from the plan is structurally inapplicable to ISF. Similarly for `pass1_container`, the set is always the fixed container criteria `{G3, G4}`. Without an explicit definition, an implementer cannot know whether to set `cohort=["ISF"]`, `cohort=[]`, `cohort=None`, or skip the field for these paths.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 'What (corrected code targets)': 'For pass1_isf, cohort=["ISF"]; for pass1_container, cohort=sorted(valid_ids) (always ["G3","G4"] today). Both follow the same sorted-criterion-id-set convention as pass1_chunk.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `cohort` should be stamped only on `pass1_chunk` findings (where co-residency is meaningful) and left as `null` for container/ISF paths, OR define the intended semantics of `cohort` for container/ISF paths explicitly and update AC3's query to filter/annotate by finder path so the contamination rate is not contaminated by synthetic cohorts.
findings.finding: The plan requires stamping `cohort` on all three finder paths (pass1_chunk, pass1_container, pass1_isf) to avoid a 'silently partial' sidecar, but the contamination-rate goal — measuring whether a finding on criterion A co-resided in a chunk with criteria B, C — is only meaningful for pass1_chunk. Container findings always have cohort=['G3','G4'] (the container set is fixed, never variable) and ISF findings always have cohort=['ISF'] (single-criterion path with no chunking). Stamping these paths doesn't enable bin-packing contamination analysis; it populates the cohort field with a synthetic value that doesn't represent co-residency. AC1's 'non-null for all' assertion would accept these synthetic values as proof, and AC3's contamination-rate script would need to filter them out anyway — but the plan doesn't specify this, leaving the offline query ambiguous.
findings.location: What (corrected code targets) section; AC1; AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Stamp `cohort` only in pass1_chunk (where `ids` represents actual co-resident criteria). For container findings, the 'cohort' is always the fixed container set — it adds no signal for contamination analysis. For ISF, there is no chunking at all. AC1 should assert `non-null for chunk-path findings`; AC3's script should filter on a `_finder_path` tag or equivalent. If the decision is to stamp all three paths anyway (for uniformity), the plan must state a positive rationale and AC3 must filter appropriately.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update AC3's pytest proof-command to point at the existing `tests/unit/test_sidecar_enrichment.py` (or explicitly justify creating a new `test_plan_review_sidecar.py` and confirm it won't duplicate sidecar fixture setup).
findings.finding: AC3's proof command references `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py`, but this file does not exist in the repo; `tests/unit/test_sidecar_enrichment.py` already covers `_slim`/`build_payload` behavior and is the established home for sidecar tests. Introducing a new test module for a single-field addition creates unnecessary test surface proliferation without justification.
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC3's proof to: `pytest tests/unit/test_sidecar_enrichment.py -k cohort` and add the cohort assertion to that file alongside the existing `_slim` tests.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC3's proof command to reference the correct existing test file (`tests/unit/test_sidecar_enrichment.py`) or explicitly call out that a new `tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py` must be created as part of this work.
findings.finding: The test file named in AC3's proof command (`tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py`) does not exist in the repository; the actual sidecar test file is `tests/unit/test_sidecar_enrichment.py`. Running the stated proof command will immediately fail with a file-not-found error.
findings.suggested_fix: Either update the pytest command in AC3 to `pytest tests/unit/test_sidecar_enrichment.py` (if cohort tests will be added there) or add a plan step: 'Create `tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py` with cohort-stamp assertions for all three finder paths.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit unit-test cases (in `test_plan_review_sidecar.py` or a new fixture) that assert the COHORT VALUE — not just non-null presence — for at least one finding produced by `pass1_container` and one by `pass1_isf`, verifying the sorted criterion-id set matches the expected chunk co-residents.
findings.finding: The test plan does not specify new test cases that assert the CORRECTNESS of the cohort value (sorted criterion-id set content) for findings from pass1_container and pass1_isf — only the jq non-null check is called out. The plan itself flags these two paths as the primary silent-partial risk ('container/ISF findings would lack cohort'), yet the acceptance criterion only proves presence (non-null), not correctness of the value. A cohort stamped as [] or with the wrong criteria set would pass the acceptance check silently.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 (`jq '.findings[].cohort' <sidecar>` non-null) and AC3 (`pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py`)
findings.suggested_fix: In AC3, extend the pytest target to include: (a) a fixture that synthesizes a container-path finding and asserts `finding['cohort'] == sorted([...expected_criteria...])`, and (b) the same for an ISF-path finding. Separately, tighten the jq proof in AC1 to `jq '[.findings[].cohort | length > 0] | all'` to at least rule out empty-list false-positives.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the AC or implementation notes how the contamination-rate script handles findings with absent/null cohort (e.g., skip with a warning, treat as unknown, or require a corpus backfill).
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how offline analysis consumers (the contamination-rate script and any existing sidecar corpus) handle old sidecar files that lack the cohort field entirely, creating a potential KeyError/silent-zero in the contamination rate computation.
findings.location: AC line 3 ('Offline analysis can compute a blocking-tier contamination rate') / What section ('Out-of-plan_review consumers')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command to AC2 that confirms the schema note exists in a named file, e.g.: `grep -q 'cohort' docs/sidecar_schema.md` or `grep -q 'cohort' CLAUDE.md`, and settle 'schema note / CLAUDE.md' to a single authoritative target.
findings.finding: AC2 ('Sidecar schema note added for the cohort field') names no proving command — no grep, no doc-build check, no file path — so an agent cannot confirm completion without subjective inspection.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria - AC2: 'Sidecar schema note added for the cohort field.'
findings.suggested_fix: Pick one artifact (e.g., CLAUDE.md) and add: Proof: `grep 'cohort' CLAUDE.md` outputs the field definition.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC4 ('Unblocks fort-wisp-wren') with a concrete, in-session observable: e.g., 'fort-wisp-wren's design doc cites a contamination-rate measurement produced by the AC3 script' OR drop it as an AC and move it to the Why section as motivation.
findings.finding: AC4 ('Unblocks fort-wisp-wren (R-1 part 2)') is not a specific observable outcome and has no proving command; it is a strategic intent claim that cannot be verified in-session.
findings.suggested_fix: Move 'Unblocks fort-wisp-wren (R-1 part 2)' to the Why section as context, and replace the AC with a verifiable prerequisite check such as: 'fort-wisp-wren issue references the contamination-rate script output as its baseline measurement.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the form and committed path of the contamination-rate analysis artifact in AC3, e.g.: 'a script at `scripts/contamination_rate.py` committed to the repo; running it against the test sidecar corpus in `tests/fixtures/` prints a numeric rate.'
findings.finding: AC3's 'a small script/query over sidecar corpus prints the rate' is a placeholder that defers creation of the script to the executor without specifying its location, form, or permanence as a repo artifact.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria - AC3: 'Offline analysis can compute a blocking-tier contamination rate... Proof: a small script/query over sidecar corpus prints the rate'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Proof: `python scripts/contamination_rate.py tests/fixtures/sample_sidecar.json` prints a float contamination rate. The script is committed to the repo.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback for `cohort` in `_slim` — either `f.get('cohort', None)` (explicit null preserved) or assert/warn when absent — so AC1's 'non-null for all' proof condition is enforceable and the offline contamination query handles missing values gracefully.
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for findings that reach `_slim` without a `cohort` key — e.g., findings produced before this change is deployed, findings from a finder path where stamping was missed, or findings from a future finder path added after this WS. The plan says `_slim` must have `cohort` in its allowlist but does not specify whether `cohort` should default to `None`, `[]`, or raise on absence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for the two named out-of-tree callers (e.g., `pytest tests/unit/test_eval_solver.py tests/unit/test_criterion_preview.py` or equivalent) or explicitly state that `test_plan_review_sidecar.py` already covers their call paths with a rationale.
findings.finding: 'check the two out-of-tree callers still pass' is bundled into AC3 but the named pytest command (`pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py`) may not cover eval_solver.py and criterion_preview.py callers; there is no stated test that exercises those two files specifically.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria - AC3: '...check the two out-of-tree callers still pass. `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Also run `pytest tests/unit/test_eval_solver.py tests/unit/test_criterion_preview.py` to confirm the caller-compatibility claim, or add integration assertions to `test_plan_review_sidecar.py` that invoke the cohort-stamped path via these callers.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale for the cohort-stamping approach: either (a) 'cohort is stamped on all paths for schema uniformity; offline queries must filter by finder_path to isolate chunk co-residency' or (b) 'cohort is stamped only on pass1_chunk findings where co-residency is defined; container/ISF findings carry cohort=null as a correct sentinel'.
findings.finding: The plan states a negative rationale for stamping cohort on all three paths ('or the sidecar is silently partial') but provides no positive rationale for why container/ISF contamination is measurable or why uniform cohort presence is preferable to a path-specific null. The absence of a positive rationale makes it impossible to judge whether the approach is intentionally uniform (for simplicity/uniformity) or inadvertently conflating availability with meaningfulness.
findings.location: What (corrected code targets) section — rationale paragraph

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC4 with a verifiable criterion, e.g. 'contamination rate is computable from sidecar output (proven by AC3 script)' or demote it to a note in Why and remove it from the AC list.
findings.finding: AC4 ('Unblocks fort-wisp-wren (R-1 part 2)') has no verification step and names no testable outcome deliverable within this ticket's scope — it cannot be confirmed as done by any action described in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify or remove the 'only a chunk COUNT survives' premise: grep `chunk_count` (or equivalent) in `sidecar.py`/`passes.py` and either cite the actual field name or restate the premise as 'no chunk co-residency data survives at all'.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'only a chunk COUNT survives' in the sidecar as justification for why cohort is currently unmeasurable, but no 'chunk_count' field (or equivalent) is found anywhere in sidecar.py or passes.py. The basis for this claim cannot be verified.
findings.location: Why section: 'throws away chunk co-residency (only a chunk COUNT survives)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'only a chunk COUNT survives' with 'no chunk co-residency data is persisted at all in the sidecar' if no count field is found, or cite the actual field name if one exists under a different key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a runtime assertion or warning log when a finding is emitted without a cohort field (or with cohort=null) so silent partial-stamping is surfaced at write time, not discovered via post-hoc jq.
findings.finding: The plan identifies 'silently partial' sidecar data as a risk (container/ISF findings lacking cohort) but specifies no detection mechanism or alerting when cohort is null/missing on an emitted finding — operators cannot distinguish a correctly-null cohort from a missed code path.
findings.location: What (corrected code targets) / AC line 1 ('jq .findings[].cohort non-null for all')
findings.suggested_fix: In each of the three finder paths (pass1_chunk, pass1_container, pass1_isf), after stamping cohort, assert `cohort is not None` and log a WARNING with the finding id and path name if it is — this converts a silent data error into an observable event.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit proof for AC3's out-of-tree caller check: either name a separate test file/function that exercises eval_solver.py and criterion_preview.py with the new finding shape, or confirm test_plan_review_sidecar.py imports and exercises those callers directly.
findings.finding: AC3's proof mechanism (pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py) does not verify the described deliverable of checking the two named out-of-tree callers (eval_solver.py, criterion_preview.py). The What section explicitly says these callers 'must be checked,' but the AC3 verification step references a sidecar unit test that covers sidecar behavior, not caller compatibility — creating a cross-section contradiction between the What decomposition and the AC3 proof.
findings.location: What section ('Out-of-plan_review consumers must be checked') vs. AC3 proof ('pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py')
findings.suggested_fix: Either extend test_plan_review_sidecar.py to import and call the relevant code paths in eval_solver.py and criterion_preview.py with a cohort-stamped finding, or add a second pytest target (e.g. tests/unit/test_pass1_callers.py) to AC3's proof line.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what `cohort` value `pass1_isf` findings should carry (e.g., `['ISF']`, a singleton, or `null`/excluded), given that ISF has no rubric chunk — the current plan treats all three finders symmetrically but `pass1_isf` has a structurally different output dict and no chunk criterion set.
findings.finding: The plan states cohort must be stamped on 'findings from pass1_chunk AND pass1_container AND pass1_isf', but pass1_isf's output dict (passes.py:451–461) is structurally asymmetric: it omits 'location' and 'checklist_item' keys that pass1_chunk/pass1_container include. Cohort stamping for ISF findings requires accounting for this different shape, which the plan does not acknowledge.
findings.location: What (corrected code targets) — 'Stamp all three or the sidecar is silently partial'; AC line 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sizing.py` (two `pass1_chunk` call sites at lines 221 and 236) to the enumerated consumers list and confirm the cohort stamp is compatible with the size-ladder escalation path.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sizing.py` calls `passes.pass1_chunk` at two sites (lines 221 and 236, within the context-limit size-ladder) but is not enumerated as a consumer in the plan's 'Out-of-plan_review consumers' bullet or elsewhere. If the cohort stamp changes the return shape of pass1_chunk in any way that requires callers to handle the new field, this path is silently unreviewed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that `cohort` is an orchestrator-injected key (like `_agentic`/`_container_child`) added in the `out.append(...)` dict-building block of each finder path — NOT a new field in the `plan_review_findings` Pydantic schema the LLM emits.
findings.finding: The plan references stamping a `cohort` field on findings from all three finder paths, but never specifies whether `cohort` is injected as (a) a new field in the `plan_review_findings` Pydantic output schema the LLM emits, or (b) a purely orchestrator-side key appended by the Python dict-building code in each finder path after LLM response parsing. The `_pass1_model` contract (passes.py:81-114) currently has no `cohort` field; if `cohort` is meant to be LLM-emitted it requires a schema change, but if it is orchestrator-injected (like `_agentic`, `_container_child`, `_container_bin`) then only the dict-building `out.append(...)` blocks need updating. This is a processing-protocol ambiguity the implementer must resolve before touching any code.
findings.location: What (corrected code targets) section; passes.py:294-306 (pass1_chunk out.append), passes.py:388-405 (pass1_container out.append), passes.py:451-461 (pass1_isf out.append); _pass1_model at passes.py:81-114
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line note to 'What (corrected code targets)': 'cohort is injected by the orchestrator dict-building block (like _agentic), NOT added to the plan_review_findings Pydantic contract — the LLM cannot self-report chunk membership.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
